commonIssues: Highly susceptible to powdery mildew; water at the base, never overhead, and ensure good air circulation (UMN, Clemson, Missouri Botanical Garden).
Also prone to Alternaria and bacterial leaf spot, root rots, blight and Botrytis; choose disease-resistant cultivars in hot, humid climates (Missouri Botanical Garden).
Overwatering and wet foliage promote fungal disease; only water when the top 1 to 2 inches of soil are dry (UMN).
Frost-tender annual: do not direct-sow until soil reaches ~70F and all frost danger has passed; killed by frost (UMN, Clemson).
Container minimum (3 L) is sized for a single dwarf-to-mid plant in a 6-inch/15-20 cm-deep pot; group plantings or tall cultivars need larger pots (Old Farmer's Almanac).

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. Full sun 8+ hours; water only when the top 1-2 inches of soil are dry; well-drained soil; general-purpose fertilizer at planting then monthly with higher phosphorus; direct-sow at soil temp >=70F (transplants at 60F), germination in 4-8 days at 70-75F; highly susceptible to powdery mildew; pH 5.5-7.5.
University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. 'Only water when the top 1 to 2 inches of soil are dry' and avoid overwatering, which promotes fungal disease; well-drained soil.
University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. General-purpose fertilizer (equal N-P-K) at planting, then monthly applications with higher phosphorus to encourage blooming.
University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. Direct seeding requires soil temp >=70F; transplants at soil 60F; performs best in a very warm location; frost-tender annual planted after last frost.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — How to Grow Zinnias. Full sun (6+ hours); water at the base, 1 inch per week, avoid overhead watering; sow when soil/air ~70F after last frost; spacing 8-9 in (dwarf) to 12 in (tall); susceptible to powdery mildew, Alternaria leaf spot, bacterial leaf spot; height 6 in to 4 ft.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — How to Grow Zinnias. Full sun (6+ hours of direct sunlight).

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Zinnia elegans. Full sun; medium moisture, humusy, evenly moist, well-drained soils; bushy/leafy upright annual; height 1-4 ft; bloom June to frost; USDA zones 2-11; susceptible to powdery mildew, leaf spots, root rots, blight and Botrytis; not frost-hardy, plant after last frost.
Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Zinnia elegans. Growth: bushy, leafy annual with upright, hairy, branching stems; spread 0.75-1.0 ft; not a climber/trailer (low vertical suitability).

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Zinnia elegans. Susceptible to powdery mildew, leaf spots, root rots, blight and Botrytis; disease-resistant cultivars recommended for hot, humid climates; good air circulation and deadheading help.

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Zinnias. Container guidance: one zinnia in a 6-inch pot, three in a 10-inch pot, up to five in a 14-inch pot; pot depth minimum 15-20 cm (6-8 in); container feeding with water-soluble 5-10-5; seedlings can be fed 5-10-5 liquid fertilizer.

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Zinnias. Feeding: 5-10-5 fertilizer at seedling stage and again mid-season; container plants fed weekly with diluted 5-10-5 (basis for preferredNpk and containerLeachAdjustment).

Estimated/conservative values. baseIntervalDays=3 derives from UMN/Clemson '1 inch per week, water when top 1-2 in dry' translated to a typical 3-day check interval; hotWeatherMultiplier=1.5 and rainSkipThresholdMm=12 are conservative defaults not given verbatim by sources. coldHardyMinC=10 and heatStressC=35 are inferred warm-season-annual bounds (sources state >=70F/21C preferred, frost-killed) rather than published thresholds. Container liters converted from imperial pot sizes (6-14 in pots ~ 3-19 L). Planting windows by zone are illustrative (zone 5 ~May-Jun, zone 7 ~Apr-Jun) consistent with after-last-frost guidance.
